RULING:
INTERVENOR FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY, THE INSURER OF DEFENDANT ASSOCIATED INSURANCE OF CALIFORNIA, INC., A SUSPENDED CORPORATION'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA, INC.

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's motion to set aside default judgment is granted. Fireman's Fund is allowed to bring said motion. (See Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196.) The default judgment entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on July 7, 2015 is set aside pursuant to the court's inherent, equitable power to set aside a default on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake. The Court further ordered continued the hearing to October 11, 2016 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503 to allow parties to meet and confer on a proposed order re: Fireman's Fund's motion to set aside the amended default entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on March 19, 2012. APPEARANCES:
Katie McClelland-Glenn, Esq., of ARMSTRONG & ASSOCIATES, LLP (510-433-1830) appearing for defendant / moving party Exxon Mobil Corporation
Chris Hersom, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Jack Harris

RULING:
DEFENDANT EXXON MOBIL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Exxon Mobil Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. As to the causation issue, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's responses to defendant's special interrogatories and Mr. Mingram's deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant had sustained its burden, Mr. Mingram's declaration creates a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. As to duty, defendant's separate statement failed to properly make an issue of whether defendant owed a duty to plaintiff for affirmative acts of its employees. Even if defendant had properly made duty an issue, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that defendant owed no duty to plaintiff. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L.
